What's going on guys in this video? We are going to be talking about General stores versus niche stores along with scaling up through Facebook ads So first off a niche store is pretty much just selling around one type of industry whether it be you know pets babies Jewelry and a general store in a sense is just like Amazon So I really like niche stores just because you're able to talk to the customer a lot Definitely and really build a brand around that where a general store like I said It's kind of like Amazon and Amazon is pretty much dominated the market. However, if you're just starting with drop shipping I do recommend you do a general store just so you can test what products sell the best for you Like there's so many products you can throw into a general store And that's the one really benefit about it is one day you can start selling jewelry in it the next day You can start selling baby things, you know, obviously you have to categorize all of those things But then you can test more so my advice would be to go out there Create a general store to start and then once you really find a product and what you're selling well Create a niche store without being your key hook product to actually lead in all of your customers Alright, so from there It's pretty simple. I'm going to click on create and obviously we have our objective now when it comes to e-commerce I always start out with conversions as my objective just a simple purchase conversion because that's what we're really trying to optimize around Anyways, if you're really building a brand though, you you know when you start getting more content and videos You do want to run some engagement campaigns, but to start if you're just testing just start with conversion purchase campaign campaigns All right from here Obviously we have our ad sets where all of the demographic goes and we're gonna just talk about a few things inside here if you don't already You know have to use it in the past one of the key things right here when it comes to interest is Stacking your interests. ## [03:01 - 06:02]

Which will really help narrow down where your customers really are So that's one of the biggest things when it comes to billing out ad sets and another thing that is really critical Especially in the beginning stages with all the testing you're gonna be doing is blanket testing all of your ad sets So what that means here? I'm gonna come back to the ads manager And I'm gonna go to an old campaign I started and you guys can see here. This was just for An old swimwear store. I actually had If I click here and I go to my ad sets you guys will see tons of different ad sets that I was actually targeting So this is what I like to call blanket testing You're really testing out every possible demographic out there That would be a potential customer that you think would you know like to buy your product and where I actually find all of these Customers are in the audience inside section of Facebook. So we're gonna go over there right now Let me just duplicate this page And I'm gonna show you what I would actually do when I'm starting out selling a product So like I said, this was just a basic swimwear store. It's audience insights up here So we're gonna just type in a broad interest once we get here And then we're gonna see a bunch of other pages that Facebook actually Recommends us. So the demographic for this would be about 18 to 25 years old and they would be interested in Let me type in Bikini, okay So women 18 to 25 that are interested in a bikini and then you can literally just click on page likes and you can see all these Other brands that they're interested in and these are things that you would never think to even target I mean, they have silly bands that those old little wristbands people wore they have pack sun So then what I would do is come back into my ad manager after I've you know, created that first ad set and Then I would just pretty much start duplicating these ad sets and I'd create at least 10 of them So $10 or excuse me 10 ad sets at $5 each to start So you're gonna be spending $50 a day But within the first week if you can just test and put up the capital upfront to really do this You guys will be able to find, you know, a product much faster That's matched with a really good demographic much faster and then from there. ## [06:02 - 09:03]

But it is a women's website That seems like it's gonna be very similar to our actual demographic. We're trying to target See that pops up So there's 240,000 people in that Actual interest which is kind of small but really not that bad I like to target anywhere from about a quarter million all the way to two million So this will be at the low-end range, but this could be a really good interest just to target by itself So what we're gonna do here is Actually just delete this The ad set name so you switch that and this is really how you start making these blanket tests inside of your ad set So now you can see just keep stacking them up and as you start getting more data on your pixel add to carts purchases You can start creating look-alike audiences I would suggest you start with add to carts first just because you're gonna have a lot more people adding to the cart at first You can create a one a one a two percent look like off of people just adding you know to the cart And from there you can start pretty much just adding on to this campaign with more and more ad sets And then one critical thing I want to show you guys here before we would just review and publish I want to come to the actual ad Okay, because this is something a lot of people miss out on and this really kind of screws up their Their ads really because they're duplicating their ads multiple times when they should be using the same exact page post ID So what that means is every time we're duplicating the ad set Well, it's duplicating the exact ad as well And what you really want is to have one key ad that gets all of the engagement Okay, because if you have let's just say 10 ads out there You're gonna have 10 different ads getting a bunch of different likes and shares and engagement when you could really be Bundling them up all into one ad. So if you come here into the upper excuse me up left corner You can go to page post and then as soon as you create your first ad you can actually find that page post ID right here So for example, I would just copy this And I come back to the ad that we're duplicating and I would make sure that it has the page post ID if not, it's just gonna say create ad and it's gonna have that Original ad that we actually created so all you have to do is click on use existing posts And then you'd be able to enter that post ID right in there And then all of your traffic would start going to pretty much that same exact ad which is super critical Especially because Facebook likes that they like when your ads are getting a lot of engagement So it doesn't make any sense to have like 10 different ads out there getting a bunch of different engagement when they could all be going Into one.
